sections.label: Section 407
sections.original: ἐκ φασμάτων δὲ τάδε νοσεῖς · ποίων ὕπο ;
sections.translation: You are sick from phantom shapes ; what sort ?

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: ἔδοξʼ ἰδεῖν τρεῖς νυκτὶ προσφερεῖς κόρας .
sections.translation: I seemed to see three maidens , black as night .

sections.label: Section 409
sections.original: οἶδʼ ἃς ἔλεξας , ὀνομάσαι δʼ οὐ βούλομαι .
sections.translation: I know whom you mean , but I do not want to name them .

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: σεμναὶ γάρ · εὐπαίδευτα δʼ ἀπετρέπου λέγειν .
sections.translation: Yes , for they are revered ; you were well-informed , to avoid naming them .

sections.label: Section 411
sections.original: αὗταί σε βακχεύουσι συγγενῆ φόνον ;
sections.translation: Are these the ones that drive you to frenzy , with the curse of kindred blood ?

sections.label: Section 412
sections.original: οἴμοι διωγμῶν , οἷς ἐλαύνομαι τάλας .
sections.translation: Oh ! the torment I endure from their pursuit !

sections.label: Section 413
sections.original: οὐ δεινὰ πάσχειν δεινὰ τοὺς εἰργασμένους .
sections.translation: It is not strange , if those who have done dreadful things should suffer them .

sections.label: Section 414
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστιν ἡμῖν ἀναφορὰ τῆς συμφορᾶς .
sections.translation: But I have a way to recover from these troubles .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: μὴ θάνατον εἴπῃς · τοῦτο μὲν γὰρ οὐ σοφόν .
sections.translation: Do not speak of death ; that is not wise .

sections.label: Section 416
sections.original: Φοῖβος , κελεύσας μητρὸς ἐκπρᾶξαι φόνον .
sections.translation: It is Phoebus , who commanded me to kill my mother .

sections.label: Section 417
sections.original: ἀμαθέστερός γʼ ὢν τοῦ καλοῦ καὶ τῆς δίκης .
sections.translation: Showing a strange ignorance of what is fair and right .

sections.label: Section 418
sections.original: δουλεύομεν θεοῖς , ὅ τι ποτʼ εἰσὶν οἱ θεοί .
sections.translation: We are slaves to the gods , whatever those gods are .

sections.label: Section 419
sections.original: κᾆτʼ οὐκ ἀμύνει Λοξίας τοῖς σοῖς κακοῖς ;
sections.translation: And does Loxias not help your affliction ?

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: μέλλει · τὸ θεῖον δʼ ἐστὶ τοιοῦτον φύσει .
sections.translation: He will in time ; this is the nature of gods .

sections.label: Section 421
sections.original: πόσον χρόνον δὲ μητρὸς οἴχονται πνοαί ;
sections.translation: How long is it since your mother breathed her last ?

sections.label: Section 422
sections.original: ἕκτον τόδʼ ἦμαρ · ἔτι πυρὰ θερμὴ τάφου .
sections.translation: This is the sixth day ; her funeral pyre is still warm .

sections.label: Section 423
sections.original: ὡς ταχὺ μετῆλθόν σʼ αἷμα μητέρος θεαί .
sections.translation: How soon the goddesses arrived to avenge your mother ’ s blood !

sections.label: Section 424
sections.original: οὐ σοφός , ἀληθὴς δʼ ἐς φίλους ἔφυν φίλος .
sections.translation: I am not clever , but I am by nature a true friend to my friends .

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: πατρὸς δὲ δή τι σʼ ὠφελεῖ τιμωρία ;
sections.translation: Does your father give you any help at all , for your avenging him ?

sections.label: Section 426
sections.original: οὔπω · τὸ μέλλον δʼ ἴσον ἀπραξίᾳ λέγω .
sections.translation: Not yet ; I call delay the equal of inaction .

sections.label: Section 427
sections.original: τὰ πρὸς πόλιν δὲ πῶς ἔχεις δράσας τάδε ;
sections.translation: How do you stand in the city after that deed of yours ?

sections.label: Section 428
sections.original: μισούμεθʼ οὕτως ὥστε μὴ προσεννέπειν .
sections.translation: I am so hated that no one will speak to me .

sections.label: Section 429
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἥγνισαι σὸν αἷμα κατὰ νόμον χεροῖν ;
sections.translation: Have your hands not even been cleaned of blood , according to custom ?

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: ἐκκλῄομαι γὰρ δωμάτων ὅποι μόλω .
sections.translation: No , for wherever I go , the door is shut against me .

sections.label: Section 431
sections.original: τίνες πολιτῶν ἐξαμιλλῶνταί σε γῆς ;
sections.translation: Which citizens are driving you from the land ?

sections.label: Section 432
sections.original: Οἴαξ , τὸ Τροίας μῖσος ἀναφέρων πατρί .
sections.translation: Oeax , who refers to my father his reason for hating Troy .

sections.label: Section 433
sections.original: συνῆκα · Παλαμήδους σε τιμωρεῖ φόνου .
sections.translation: I understand ; he is avenging on you the blood of Palamedes .

sections.label: Section 434
sections.original: οὗ γʼ οὐ μετῆν μοι · διὰ τριῶν δʼ ἀπόλλυμαι .
sections.translation: That was nothing to do with me ; yet I am destroyed for three reasons .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἄλλος ; ἦ που τῶν ἀπʼ Αἰγίσθου φίλων ;
sections.translation: Who else ? Some of the friends of Aegisthus , I suppose ?

sections.label: Section 436
sections.original: οὗτοί μʼ ὑβρίζουσʼ , ὧν πόλις τὰ νῦν κλύει .
sections.translation: They insult me , and the city listens to them now .

sections.label: Section 437
sections.original: Ἀγαμέμνονος δὲ σκῆπτρʼ ἐᾷ σʼ ἔχειν πόλις ;
sections.translation: Will the city allow you to keep the scepter of Agamemnon ?

sections.label: Section 438
sections.original: πῶς , οἵτινες ζῆν οὐκ ἐῶσʼ ἡμᾶς ἔτι ;
sections.translation: How , seeing that they will not allow me to remain alive ?

sections.label: Section 439
sections.original: τί δρῶντες ὅ τι καὶ σαφὲς ἔχεις εἰπεῖν ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: What is their method ? Can you tell me plainly ?

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: ψῆφος καθʼ ἡμῶν οἴσεται τῇδʼ ἡμέρᾳ .
sections.translation: A vote will be taken against us today .

sections.label: Section 441
sections.original: φεύγειν πόλιν τήνδʼ ; ἢ θανεῖν ἢ μὴ θανεῖν ;
sections.translation: To leave the city ? Or to die , or not to die ?

sections.label: Section 442
sections.original: θανεῖν ὑπʼ ἀστῶν λευσίμῳ πετρώματι .
sections.translation: Death by stoning at the hands of the citizens .

sections.label: Section 443
sections.original: κᾆτʼ οὐχὶ φεύγεις γῆς ὑπερβαλὼν ὅρους ;
sections.translation: Then why not cross the border and try to escape ?

sections.label: Section 444
sections.original: κύκλῳ γὰρ εἱλισσόμεθα παγχάλκοις ὅπλοις .
sections.translation: Because we are encircled by men fully armed .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ἰδίᾳ πρὸς ἐχθρῶν ἢ πρὸς Ἀργείας χερός ;
sections.translation: Private foes or Argive troops ?

sections.label: Section 446
sections.original: πάντων πρὸς ἀστῶν , ὡς θάνω · βραχὺς λόγος .
sections.translation: All the citizens , so that I may die ; it is shortly told .

sections.label: Section 447
sections.original: ὦ μέλεος , ἥκεις συμφορᾶς ἐς τοὔσχατον .
sections.translation: Poor wretch ! you have arrived at the extremity of woe .

sections.label: Section 448
sections.original: ἐς σὲ ἐλπὶς ἡμὴ καταφυγὰς ἔχει κακῶν .
sections.translation: In you I have hopes of escape from my troubles . But since you have come with good fortune ,

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: μετάδος φίλοισι σοῖσι σῆς εὐπραξίας ,
sections.translation: share with your friends , who are wretched , your prosperity ; do not hold aside that goodness for yourself alone ; but partake of troubles in your turn , and so pay back my father ’ s kindness to those who have a claim on you . For such friends as desert us in adversity

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: οἱ μὴ ʼπὶ ταῖσι συμφοραῖς ὄντες φίλοι .
sections.translation: are friends in name but not in deed .

sections.label: Section 456
sections.original: καὶ μὴν γέροντι δεῦρʼ ἁμιλλᾶται ποδὶ
sections.translation: And here is Tyndareus , the Spartan , struggling with aged step , clad in black robes , with his hair cut short in mourning for his daughter .

sections.label: Section 459
sections.original: ἀπωλόμην , Μενέλαε · Τυνδάρεως ὅδε
sections.translation: Menelaus , I am ruined . See , Tyndareus

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: στείχει πρὸς ἡμᾶς , οὗ μάλιστʼ αἰδώς μʼ ἔχει
sections.translation: approaches us , the man of all others I most shrink from facing , because of the deed I have done . For he nursed me when I was small , and lavished on me many a fond caress , carrying me about in his arms as the son of Agamemnon ; and so did Leda ;

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: τιμῶντέ μʼ οὐδὲν ἧσσον ἢ Διοσκόρω ·
sections.translation: for they both honored me no less than the Dioscuri .

sections.label: Section 466
sections.original: οἷς , ὦ τάλαινα καρδία ψυχή τʼ ἐμή ,
sections.translation: Ah me ! my wretched heart and soul , it was a sorry return I made them ! What darkness can I find for my face ? What cloud can I spread before me in my efforts to escape the old man ’ s eye ?

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: ποῦ ποῦ θυγατρὸς τῆς ἐμῆς ἴδω πόσιν ,
sections.translation: Where , where may I see Menelaus , my daughter ’ s husband ? For as I was pouring libations on Clytemnestra ’ s grave I heard that he had come to Nauplia with his wife , safe home again after many years . Lead me to him ; for I want to approach him

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: στὰς ἀσπάσασθαι , χρόνιος εἰσιδὼν φίλον .
sections.translation: and clasp his hand , as a friend whom at last I see again .

sections.label: Section 476
sections.original: ὦ πρέσβυ , χαῖρε , Ζηνὸς ὁμόλεκτρον κάρα .
sections.translation: Hail , old man , rival of Zeus for a bride !

sections.label: Section 477
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρε καὶ σύ , Μενέλεως , κήδευμʼ ἐμόν .
sections.translation: All hail to you , Menelaus , my kinsman !

sections.label: Section 478
sections.original: ἔα · τὸ μέλλον ὡς κακὸν τὸ μὴ εἰδέναι .
sections.translation: Catching sight of Orestes . Ah ! What an evil it is to be ignorant of the future ! There is that matricide before the house , a viper darting venomous flashes from his eyes , whom I loathe .

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: στίλβει νοσώδεις ἀστραπάς , στύγημʼ ἐμόν .
sections.translation: Menelaus , are you speaking to that godless wretch ?

sections.label: Section 482
sections.original: τί γάρ ; φίλου μοι πατρός ἐστιν ἔκγονος .
sections.translation: And why not ? He is the son of one whom I loved .

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: κείνου γὰρ ὅδε πέφυκε , τοιοῦτος γεγώς ;
sections.translation: This is his son , this creature here ?

sections.label: Section 484
sections.original: πέφυκεν · εἰ δὲ δυστυχεῖ , τιμητέος .
sections.translation: Yes , his son ; if he is in misfortune , he ought to be honored .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: βεβαρβάρωσαι , χρόνιος ὢν ἐν βαρβάροις .
sections.translation: You have been so long among barbarians that you have become one of them .

sections.label: Section 486
sections.original: Ἑλληνικόν τοι τὸν ὁμόθεν τιμᾶν ἀεί .
sections.translation: Always to honor one ’ s kin is a custom in Hellas .
